q: If equal amounts of charge (q) are placed at one corner of a square, then at the centre of the square the force will be ________.
Which of the following correctly represents the order of metals in the reactivity series?
In which of the following devices is electrical energy used in the form of light?
Which of the following carbon compounds shows the presence of a branched chain structure?
Which of the following best describes the components of the reproductive system of the flower in plants?
o: The petal and the sepal are involved in reproduction.
Only the male part is included in reproduction.
The androecium and gynoecium are directly involved in reproduction.
Only the female part takes part in reproduction.

q: Brass is used to make a vessel, into which a sample of an impure metal is poured. Which of the following statements about brass is correct?
o: Brass cannot be cast into different shapes and so the work can be done separately.
Brass can be poured into different shapes and so the work can be done easily.
Brass cannot be cast into different shapes only and so a single shape is shown.
Brass and pure metal have the same properties, so brass is an example of a pure metal.

q: The capacity of a single cell is measured in units of ________.
The parenchyma cells in plants are:
o: dead and elongated with lignified walls
living and thickened at corners without spaces
living and loosely arranged with intercellular spaces
dead and compactly arranged without spaces

q: What is that main resource which can lead to the maximum conservation and use among the natural resources?
o: Among the resources, only forests should be managed.
Only the management of river water is needed.
All resources should be conserved and used wisely.

q: How does asexual reproduction occur in living organisms without copying of DNA?
o: Because DNA is divided into two parts which are then copied separately.
Because DNA copies are not made and then divided.
Because DNA copies are made and then divided into two parts.
Because DNA copies are not made and then are not divided.

q: A 1 kg lump of mass X is dropped from the middle of a wall onto the ground. If the lump hits the ground at 20 m/s, then what is the height of the wall? (g = 10 m/s^2)
Which animal is used as a means of communication by making sounds?
Which of the following equations correctly represents the relationship between physical quantities for a moving object in uniformly accelerated motion?
The focal length of a concave lens is -1 m. Which of the following statements about the position of its image is correct?
o: The image is in front of the focus.
The image is between the focus and the lens.
The image is at the focus.
The image is behind the focus.

q: The resistance of any conductor depends on ________.
o: the natural protective coating on the conductor
length, cross-sectional area, nature of the material, and temperature of the conductor
only the length and cross-sectional area of the conductor

q: In which form do soap molecules organise themselves in order to carry out the cleaning action?
Which of the following cell organelles have cisterns and is involved in the formation of lysosomes?
What is the primary chemistry behind a displacement reaction?
o: Compounds disintegrate into two or more components
Two reactants combine to generate one product
Ion exchange occurs between two substances
One element displaces another from a compound

q: In a reaction, m moles of element X combine with the central atom of element B in the proportion of 2:n. If equal moles of B react with element A to form a compound with formula A2Bn, then what will be the value of the central atom n of B?
Which of the following methods provides protection from sexually transmitted diseases during sexual intercourse?
How are the waste products excreted in plants themselves?
o: By storing them in old xylem vessels in the form of resin and gum
By releasing them into the soil through the roots, dissolved in oxygen and water
Through the special excretory tissue in the leaves

q: What defines two stars as being of the same kind?
o: Both have the same surface temperature.
Both contain the same number of different chemical elements.

q: In a double-displacement reaction, what type of ion exchange occurs between the reactants?
o: Cation - cation and anion - anion both ions

q: Different stars are arranged according to the temperature ________, but the number of ________ remains the same. On what basis are they classified?
If the velocity of a body changes from +20 m/s to -10 m/s in 5 seconds, then what will be the acceleration?
In triangle ABC, point D lies on BC. Angle ABC = 71 degrees. BC is extended to a point E such that angle CAE = 55 degrees. What is the measure of angle AEB?
A person saves 75% of his monthly income and so saves Rs 4,827 per month. Find his monthly income (in Rs).
Find the simple interest on Rs 2,000 at 7% per annum for the period from 3 February 2023 to 17 April 2023.
If 25, 8 and P are in continued proportion, then find the value of P.

Tarun goes from his house to his office walking at his normal speed and takes 19 minutes to reach. If he walks at 3/4 of his normal speed, how much time will he take to cover the same distance going from his house to his office?
A man buys an item for Rs 16 each at a wholesale price. If he sells each of them for Rs 2.8, then find his profit percentage.
The present ages of two persons Gopal and Manoj differ by 2 years. If the ratio of their present ages will be 21:22 after 18 years, then find the sum of their ages 10 years ago (in years).
A trader marks the marked price of his goods 37% above the cost price and gives a discount of 16% on the marked price. What is his profit or loss percentage?

The length of the two parallel sides of an isosceles trapezium are 11.9 cm and a cm, where the smaller side a is 8 cm, and the distance between the two smaller sides is 3.9 cm and 5.1 cm; then find the perimeter of the isosceles trapezium.

From a point on the ground exactly below the top of a 50 m high tower, the angle of elevation of the top of the tower is 30 degrees, and from a point standing upright the angle of elevation is 60 degrees. Find the height of the tower from the ground (in metres).
Which of the following numbers is divisible by both 2 and 5?

A water tank can be filled in 6 minutes, whereas the second pipe can completely empty the full tank in 15 minutes. If both the pipes are opened together when the tank is empty, in how many minutes will half the tank be filled?
A trader buys a washing machine for Rs 7660. If he gives a discount of 12% on it and even then makes a profit of 10%, then find the marked price of the machine.

The monthly incomes of two persons Rakesh and Shyam are in the ratio 5:6 and each of their expenditures is in the ratio 1:3. If each of them saves Rs 39,000, then find Rakesh's monthly income (in Rs).

Two trains, whose lengths are 248 m and 142 m respectively, are running in opposite directions. The first train runs at 34 km/hr and the second train runs at 74 km/hr. How much time will they take to cross each other?
